title: English – Critical Reasoning Focus, Set 2 (Grade 5 Ontario)
subject: english

passages.title: James Naismith and the First Basketball Game
passages.body:
In December 1891, James Naismith, a physical education instructor at a YMCA training school in Springfield, Massachusetts, faced a problem. His students were restless indoors during the winter, and rougher games led to injuries. He needed a new activity that would keep players moving but limit aggressive contact.

Naismith wrote thirteen simple rules and hung two peach baskets at opposite ends of the gym — one for each team. The ball had to be thrown into the opponent's basket to score; running with the ball was not allowed. The first game used a soccer ball, and someone had to climb a ladder to retrieve the ball after each score until the bottoms of the baskets were eventually cut out.

Naismith was born in Almonte, Ontario, and studied in Montreal before moving to the United States. The sport he invented spread quickly through YMCAs and colleges, and later around the world. Today basketball is played professionally and in school gyms, and the rules have grown more detailed — but the basic idea of passing, shooting, and teamwork remains his legacy.

questions.prompt: According to the passage, why did Naismith want a new indoor activity?
choices: Because winter games were causing injuries and students needed something safer
Because peach baskets were easy to find
questions.answer: Because winter games were causing injuries and students needed something safer

questions.prompt: What did players use as goals in the first game?
choices: Metal hoops with nets from the start
questions.answer: Peach baskets

questions.prompt: What problem with baskets does the passage mention?
choices: Someone had to retrieve the ball after each score until the bottoms were cut out
They were only used in Canada
questions.answer: Someone had to retrieve the ball after each score until the bottoms were cut out

questions.prompt: Where was James Naismith born, according to the passage?
questions.answer: Almonte, Ontario

questions.prompt: The last sentence says the "basic idea" of basketball "remains Naismith's legacy." "Legacy" here is closest in meaning to —
choices: lasting gift or influence left behind
questions.answer: lasting gift or influence left behind
questions.hintContext: A legacy is what someone leaves for future generations.

questions.prompt: Which sentence uses a metaphor?
choices: The river of homework never ended.
questions.answer: The river of homework never ended.

questions.prompt: A survey asked only people leaving a comic book store, "Do you prefer comics to novels?" Why might the results misrepresent the whole town?
choices: Because the sample probably includes many people who already like comics
questions.answer: Because the sample probably includes many people who already like comics

questions.prompt: "She negotiated firmly but politely." The word "firmly" suggests she was —
choices: clear and steady in her position
questions.answer: clear and steady in her position

questions.prompt: Which pair are antonyms (opposites)?
questions.answer: generous / stingy

questions.prompt: Read: "Although the trail was steep, we reached the summit." The word "Although" signals —
questions.answer: a contrast

questions.prompt: Which is the best thesis for a paragraph about reducing food waste at school?
choices: Our school can reduce food waste by composting, smaller portions, and education.
questions.answer: Our school can reduce food waste by composting, smaller portions, and education.

questions.prompt: If all squares are rectangles, and this shape is not a rectangle, what is valid?
questions.answer: It cannot be a square
questions.hintContext: Squares are a type of rectangle. If a shape is not a rectangle, can it be a square?

questions.prompt: The instructions were ambiguous." "Ambiguous" means —
choices: unclear or open to more than one meaning
questions.answer: unclear or open to more than one meaning

questions.prompt: Which closing fits a persuasive letter to the principal about a school garden?
choices: Sincerely, we hope you will consider our proposal.
questions.answer: Sincerely, we hope you will consider our proposal.

questions.prompt: A headline reads: "Local Team Crushes Rivals!" This is an example of —
questions.answer: loaded or emotional language

questions.prompt: Which revision is written in consistent past tense?
choices: She runs to the store and bought milk.
She ran to the store and bought milk.
She run to the store and buys milk.
She running to the store and bought milk.
questions.answer: She ran to the store and bought milk.

questions.prompt: "The data contradicts the earlier study." "Contradicts" means —
questions.answer: conflicts with or opposes

questions.prompt: Which source is most likely primary for studying Naismith's original rules?
choices: A blog post from a fan who never read the rules
A photograph of the handwritten rules from 1891
A movie about basketball made in 2020
questions.answer: A photograph of the handwritten rules from 1891
questions.hintContext: Primary sources come directly from the time or person studied.

questions.prompt: Which word is spelled correctly?
questions.answer: definitely

questions.prompt: Choose the best topic sentence for a paragraph about active listening.
choices: Active listening means focusing on the speaker, asking questions, and summarizing what you heard.
Listening is a word with six letters.
questions.answer: Active listening means focusing on the speaker, asking questions, and summarizing what you heard.
